denial_pattern_sources: missing_iro_notice: CMS External Appeal Regulations 45 CFR § 147.136; ACA § 2719
incorrect_demographic_guideline: Commonwealth Fund 2023 Review of Prior Auth Denials
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 01/19/2026 for Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair related to Rotator cuff tear, full-thickness (M75.101). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The requested frequency exceeds plan guidelines for Rotator cuff tear, full-thickness (M75.101). Additional visits are not medically necessary at this cadence.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with frequency excessive. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Rotator cuff tear, full-thickness (M75.101). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed 14 weeks PT; MRI supraspinatus full-thickness tear 1.8 cm. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that frequency excessive bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
